The stock moved 10.4% on earnings day, likely due to the positive earnings surprise and the company's overall financial performance, although specific guidance or outlook is not provided in the text.

ORCL.summary: Oracle reported Q4 earnings per share of $2.11, beating estimates of $1.96, with record total revenues of $19.2 billion, up 21% year-over-year. The company's strong results were driven by broad-based demand for its cloud technology and applications suites, particularly in Cloud Infrastructure, which saw 93% growth. Oracle provided strong guidance for Q1 FY 2027, with total revenues expected to grow 27-29% and total cloud revenue expected to grow 57-63%, but the stock still moved -9.6% on earnings day, potentially due to high expectations and the significant capital investments required to support the growth of its Cloud Infrastructure business.

Oracle reported Q1 2026 earnings with an EPS of $1.79, beating estimates of $1.69, driven by strong demand for cloud infrastructure, AI training and inferencing, multicloud database, and cloud applications, which has given the company the opportunity to build and scale its cloud infrastructure rapidly. The company's strong performance was attributed to its ability to meet the growing demand for its services, with organic total revenue and non-GAAP earnings per share exceeding 20% growth. The stock moved up 12.7% on earnings day, likely due to the company's impressive growth momentum and the appointment of a new CFO, Hilary Maxson, who is expected to help the company continue to invest with discipline and translate its momentum into durable, long-term value for customers and shareholders.
ORCL.keyQuote: Oracle has built extraordinary momentum at the intersection of cloud, AI, and industry applications. I’m excited to join at this pivotal moment, and I look forward to partnering with Clay, Mike, and the broader leadership team to continue to invest with discipline and to translate this momentum into durable, long-term value for customers and shareholders.
